meta_description: Learn how to marketing automation and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Build Your Portfolio: Marketing Automation for Students
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' barrier by building a marketing automation tool. Learn to use Antigravity and Claude Code for a high-impact student project.
content.answer_block: To solve the 'no portfolio' trap, build a functional marketing automation engine. By automating lead collection or email workflows, you demonstrate real-world engineering value. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative development to ship a production-ready project that proves you can solve actual business problems.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the most daunting obstacle for any engineering student. You have the theoretical knowledge, but employers don't want to see another generic To-Do list; they want to see code that moves the needle for a business. The 'no portfolio' syndrome stops here.

We are going to bridge the gap between student and professional by building a high-impact marketing automation tool. This isn't just a project—it's a demonstration of your ability to integrate APIs, manage asynchronous workflows, and deliver measurable results. Let's turn your lack of experience into a competitive advantage.

steps.title: Define the Automation Logic
steps.description: Establish the scope: a tool that monitors a source (like a Google Sheet) and triggers an automated email sequence via an API.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a scaffold for a Node.js marketing automation service that connects to a Google Sheet API and triggers an email notification service.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a professional project structure with error handling and logging.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Pipeline
steps.description: Configure the connection between your data source and the automation logic to ensure reliable data ingestion.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust data ingestion module that handles rate limiting and retries for external API requests.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement a retry mechanism with exponential backoff for all outgoing API requests.'

steps.title: Implement the Action Trigger
steps.description: Write the logic that executes the marketing action, such as sending a personalized email or Slack message.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a function that processes input data and triggers a personalized email template using a mock service.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Build the email dispatcher logic with placeholder variables for personalization.'

steps.title: Add Observability
steps.description: Engineers need logs. Implement basic monitoring to track your automation's success and failure rates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate a lightweight logging system that tracks every automation event and logs errors to a local file.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Add structured logging to the pipeline to track success and failure rates.'

steps.title: Deploy and Document
steps.description: Prepare your code for the world. Create a README that highlights the business problem you solved.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a professional README.md that explains the project, the stack, and how the automation improves business efficiency.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Draft a technical README.md that emphasizes the business impact of this tool.'

pro_tips: Always focus on the 'Why' in your README; employers care about business outcomes, not just the lines of code.
Use environment variables for all API keys—hardcoding secrets is the fastest way to fail a technical interview.
Implement a 'dry-run' mode in your automation so users can test the workflow without actually sending emails.
Structure your code with clear modularity; if a recruiter reads your repo, they should see clean, maintainable patterns.
Add a 'Future Improvements' section to your README to show you have the mindset of a long-term software engineer.
content.next_steps: Once your automation is live, deploy it to a free-tier cloud service like Vercel or Render. Share the link on LinkedIn with a breakdown of the business problem you solved, and use the code as the centerpiece of your technical portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now have a project that solves a real business problem—the exact type of work that gets you hired. Don't stop here; keep iterating, keep automating, and keep building. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
